records.ety: [OE. vou, OF. vou, veu, vo, vu, F. v<?/u, from L. votum, from vovere, to vow. Cf. Avow, Devout, Vote.]

defs: A solemn promise made to God, or to some deity; an act by which one consecrates or devotes himself, absolutely or conditionally, wholly or in part, for a longer or shorter time, to some act, service, or condition; a devotion of one's possessions; as, a baptismal vow; a vow of poverty.
quotes: I pray thee, let me go and pay my vow.
I am combined by a sacred vow.

defs: Specifically, a promise of fidelity; a pledge of love or affection; as, the marriage vow.
quotes: Knights of love, who never broke their vow; Firm to their plighted faith.

records.ety: [OE. vouen, OF. vouer, voer, F. vouer, LL. votare. See Vow, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Vowed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Vowing.]

defs: To give, consecrate, or dedicate to God, or to some deity, by a solemn promise; to devote; to promise solemnly.
quotes: [Men] that vow a long and weary pilgrimage.

defs: To make a vow, or solemn promise.
quotes: Better is it that thou shouldest not vow, than that thou shouldest vow and not pay.
